summary: Tight 1987 set with ripping guitar, strong transitions and solid jams
blurb: The show delivers high‑energy playing from start to finish, highlighted by seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider and a fiery Crazy Fingers → Samson stretch. Jerry’s lead guitar is on fire while the rhythm section keeps the momentum tight. Excellent audience and matrix recordings capture the vibe with crystal‑clear sound.
review: On April 3, 1987 at The Centrum in Worcester, the Dead rolled out a businesslike, high‑energy set that feels as fresh today as it did back then. Set 1 kicks off with a punchy Hell In A Bucket and rides through a concise first half that never loses momentum. The real fireworks light up in Set 2 – China Cat Sunflower segues flawlessly into I Know You Rider, then the band launches into a Spanish‑flavored Crazy Fingers that morphs into Samson & Delilah with Jerry ripping his guitar behind Bob’s soaring vocals. A tight Drums/Space launch propels The Other One into a muscular jam before winding down with Wharf Rat, Throwing Stones and a predictable but still solid Not Fade Away. Throughout the night Jerry is “ripping it up,” delivering scorching leads that sit perfectly over Phil’s bass – albeit a tad thin on some audience tapes – while Brent’s keys provide just enough texture to keep the soundscape full. The crowd’s enthusiasm shines through especially on the excellent audience recordings, making this a must‑listen snapshot of the late‑’80s Dead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings are clearly positive and one is mixed; reviewers repeatedly praise the tight playing, standout transitions (China>Rider, Crazy Fingers>Samson) and Jerry’s ripping guitar work, with no consistent negative criticism about performance.

best_recording.reason: Excellent audience tape praised as a huge upgrade with crystal‑clear sound, no dropouts, and captures the tight, energetic performance perfectly.

key_highlights: High‑energy, businesslike flow that keeps momentum tight from start to finish.
Seamless transitions in Set 2 – China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and Crazy Fingers > Samson & Delilah.
Jerry’s ripping lead guitar work throughout, especially on the jammed songs.
Excellent audience and matrix recordings provide clear, immersive listening experience.

band_performance.Jerry: Ripping lead guitar throughout, especially on China>Rider and the Crazy Fingers>Sams​on stretch; his solos are fire‑hot and drive the set.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation, though a few listeners note it sounds a bit thin on some audience tapes.
band_performance.Bob: Strong vocal delivery, highlighted in the last verses of jammed numbers; adds energy to transitions.
